title: Chest discomfort non-acute review

red_flags.text: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.
red_flags.rationale: The exact DHA red-flag and referral section supports clinician-assessed acute or unstable features, abnormal observations, cardiorespiratory concern, and escalation documentation without asserting non-acute safety.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Chest Pain – 45

investigations.text: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.
investigations.rationale: The exact stable chest-pain section supports pain character, location, exertional relation, relieving factors, cardiovascular risk, prior investigations, and clinician-led assessment without confirming angina. The exact stable assessment section supports recording existing ECG, imaging, or test context and clinician-decided follow-up without automatically ordering investigations.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis

exact_section.heading: Section 1.3 - stable chest-pain assessment and investigation context

source_limitations: Current onset, severity, exertional pattern, observations, ECG, cardiac risk, acute features, cause, diagnosis, investigation, medicine, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
